host: Windows 11 x64, Intel Core Ultra 5 125H, 16GB RAM
notes: Go used crypto/hmac with crypto/sha256 from stdlib
Go used built-in make() with GC (no explicit free)
Rust used hmac v0.12.1 crate with sha2
Rust used serde v1.0.228 + serde_json v1.0.149
Rust used Vec<Vec<u8>> via global allocator (Windows HeapAlloc)
Rust dominates JSON: serde_json compile-time codegen vs reflection (Go) / runtime walking (Zig)
Zig JSON slow because Stringify uses dynamic dispatch + new Io.Writer.Allocating per iter
Go MEMALLOC fastest because GC bump-allocator beats glibc-style malloc for short-lived objects
Rust MEMALLOC slowest because Vec<u8> double-allocates (header + data) and Windows HeapAlloc has overhead
Zig HMAC fastest, but Rust within 4% — both stdlib crypto are optimized
SHA-256 essentially tied: Rust 85ms / Zig 90ms / Go 99ms — all use SIMD-friendly impls
All binaries compiled with native CPU detection on Intel Core Ultra 5 125H
